prompt: A lone tightrope walker balanced on a rope stretched between two rocky cliffs, viewed from the front, at golden hour. The left side of the rope sags downward under heavy invisible weight, pulling toward the left cliff — the walker leans to compensate. The right side of the rope is fraying and vibrating, individual fibers splitting outward in all directions. The center of the rope, directly under the walker's feet, is perfectly taut, straight, and calm. The walker stands still, arms outstretched, eyes forward. The background is a deep misty gorge, vast and empty. Photorealistic, cinematic lighting, sharp focus on the walker and rope, shallow depth of field blurring the cliffs. Single frame, no text, no labels, no numbers, no charts, no overlays, no infographic elements.
metaphor: A tightrope walker: the sagging left rope is high bias pulling toward oversimplification, the fraying right rope is high variance, the taut center is optimal balance.
